reviews.author: Stephen
reviews.text: Great place. Amazing view. Coconut the size of my head at least a litre of juice it just didnt empty. Fresh seafood. Must go to place. Cash or wize only

reviews.author: Vilma G.
reviews.text: It's just local village. If you're hoping to walk on the long bridge, located on the coast, you will be disappointed because it's broken at the beginning. There is not much to see in general. The restaaurants on the water is fun attraction though.

reviews.author: Keila DuSold
reviews.text: To the left of the government-dismantled pier are the floating restaurants with fresh food. I can recommend Nhà Bè Hải Sản Ngọc Hân and its small oysters. We also had large oysters, fresh crab, conch with marine snails, fried rice and coconut water. Great little area for fresh food with a beautiful ocean view.

reviews.author: Stijn Zaalberg
reviews.text: We've been here a couple of weeks back after a long and wobbly ride towards the so-called Starfish beach. Forget about the beach, the people apparently are fishing them out the sea and dropping them on the sand to dry, a shame. But the people from this place seem to be honest about their fishing profession. The fish you order from the menu is freshly made and ready within 10-30 minutes. The price is not steep for the actual plate you are receiving. There's even an "Instagram" photo spot with a chair and a little tree. (If that's your cup of tea) The host was friendly and her husband was the cook and fisherman I think.

reviews.author: Ihar Beliauski
reviews.text: Hàm Ninh Fishing Town (Làng chài Hàm Ninh) was a bit of a letdown for us. There's really nothing special about it, and it feels more like a place for locals than tourists. The area was quite dirty, and we found it lacking in charm compared to other places on the island. If you're looking for a better experience, I’d recommend heading to the main night market, which offers much nicer things to try. Another downside here was how difficult it was to find a taxi back - be prepared for a bit of a struggle if you visit.

reviews.author: Kenkeh Hi
reviews.text: Visited this town on my 1st day arrival for late lunch at Hạnh Nhung Restaurant, 14 minutes drive from the airport , so many seafood restaurant here , price cater for tourist (we were 4 ladies , cost usd35 per person for fresh seafood lunch ) and not local Phu Quốc folks ! Even fruits we bought from the street vendor cost usd25 (Rambutan,Longan,Apple custard , mangosteen )

reviews.author: misssilvya
reviews.text: I found this village particularly dirty and the fish&seafood restaurants overpriced and not very pretty. However towards the end of our trip we found a decent place to eat, local food, which we really enjoyed. What was great about this place is that they served food early in the morning, so we could have some nice breakfast there. Sadly this place doesn’t have their eatery listed on Google, but I will add a picture of it.

reviews.author: Hien Nguyen
reviews.text: This fishing village surprised me with its rustic charm. Walking along the long pier and tasting fresh crab is unforgettable. The sunset view is amazing. I’d tell my friends this is the best place for a glimpse of local life and seafood in Phu Quoc.

reviews.author: Sam Son
reviews.text: It had many small restaurants on pier, where you could see fishermen bringing live seafood straight to the shops. Customers were to choose the menu and negotiate on the price. The food was cheap and it had its own sentimental value to it. But since the pier was torn down, (only a half way) this hot tourist fishing village is not as used to be. Many local businesses have been closed down, some seafood restaurants still open on the northen side of Ham Ninh village with their own piers

reviews.author: Nándor Bacsó
reviews.text: Its just a village with lots of fresh local seafood restaurants. Theres nothing wrong with the place, but dont expect a tourist attraction =)))

reviews.author: Scott Miller
reviews.text: It was okay to go and check out but you can tell that it is slack now and there was not much going on, so still worth going to see and plenty of fish options and seafood as well to choose from.

reviews.author: Amy Mai
reviews.text: Small local market but have enough seafood s for mine craving 😋 still much cheaper than Phú Quốc Night Market. Must try floating restaurants. They will cook fresh from seafood that you pick

reviews.author: Nguyễn Thống Nhất
reviews.text: Sea foods are fresh since they are keeping them alive The boat service to the starfish beach is very convenient

reviews.author: Howard Snow
reviews.text: Quaint little town on the west coast of Phu Quoc. Lovely restaurants on the seafront serving freshly caught seafood. It’s a shame the pier has fallen down in parts as it would have been interesting to walk out to get a good view of the town from that angle.

reviews.author: Matshidiso Pitswane
reviews.text: We caught the sunrise here, so beautiful. The pier was good, it offered a perfect view of the sunrise, there are lots of floating restaurants and I imagine the whole place comes alive when the places are open. We visited super early, no one was open and well to be honest, I enjoyed the quiet, the morning before "the morning".

reviews.author: wanja van den berg
reviews.text: Over priced and not worth the trip. Not as beautify as other places on the island.

reviews.author: Arlene Lum
reviews.text: Unless I missed it, this was not an “attraction” per Se, it was just another town and didn’t see anything characteristic or photogenic about it. We are lunch in a local restaurant so we could be near the water but that was it.

reviews.author: Yann Losseau
reviews.text: Absolutely disgusting. Trash everywhere, those people absolutely does not take care of their village. Tourist should not give them any money until they learn to respect this beautiful environment.

reviews.author: Viktoria von Freiburg
reviews.text: A must visit on Phu Quoc. Don't forget to eat seafood there!

reviews.author: Minh Nguyen
reviews.text: Hot and dusty. Seafood was good and fresh but relatively expensive compared to prices in Saigon

reviews.author: Tú Nguyễn
reviews.text: Must try the seafood here. Reasonable price and very fresh.

reviews.author: MuzzDog
reviews.text: Original, rustic fishing village. Fresh seafood. Choose your own live prawns, lobsters, fish etc. Terrific young staff very helpful. Very good value too.

reviews.author: 路竹主委
reviews.text: The restaurant is not very clean, so be cautious. The prices are reasonable. However, the sea view is spoiled by a lot of trash, making it less appealing. While the place feels authentic, there’s no real reason to plan a trip here since there isn’t much to see. Most importantly, it’s not very clean.

reviews.author: gam vu
reviews.text: Honestly special to enjoy. A bit over price for sea food. Cant deny they are fresh. But not so good for money

reviews.author: Jean Poulain
reviews.text: Top but quite empty. Very good spot to eat seafood especially crabs and lobsters.

reviews.author: conrad
reviews.text: Really a fishing area, you will see everywhere every kind of alive sea life, not just fishes... Of course all that is for eating and some of the shops also cook for you express

reviews.author: Małgorzata Strzymińska
reviews.text: Nice view for the dinner. Quite tasty spring rolls and fried rice with chicken

reviews.author: Han E
reviews.text: We were told that this is the place for seafood by the hotel staffs. As a result, we headed out hoping for a grand meal on our first night in Phu Quoc. The restaurant is over the water and we had our ambiance. However, the food was average at best and the price was not worth the quality the kitchen produced.

reviews.author: Jones Yuen
reviews.text: Food was fresh and tasty, but the price was abit on the high.

reviews.author: Peter J P Lim Lim
reviews.text: A disappointing experience, poor selection of seafood and a sleepy village with no points of interest
